texto: To love, to live, to feel so much that your world keeps spinning, faster and faster, in that wonderful, chaotic mess of humanity that you’d so hastily give up. Immortality is overrated. It is nothing but the ability to live through it all and not experience a single thing, to eat everything without tasting it at all." Isak’s eyes shone with a desperate need. He wanted, more than anything it seemed, to be like me, when all I wanted was to be like him.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Sophia Whittemore: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “To love, to live, to feel so much that your world keeps spinning, faster and faster, in that wond…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amor, adventure, angsty-love pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de angsty-love e desperate-love.

frase_original: To love, to live, to feel so much that your world keeps spinning, faster and faster, in that wonderful, chaotic mess of humanity that you’d so hastily give up. Immortality is overrated. It is nothing but the ability to live through it all and not experience a single thing, to eat everything without tasting it at all." Isak’s eyes shone with a desperate need. He wanted, more than anything it seemed, to be like me, when all I wanted was to be like him.
